contextSummary: Dify's in-house design-system Toast (dify-ui), consumed across its agentic-workflow-builder product. Implements four typed tones (success/error/warning/info), a `promise()` helper mirroring sonner's API, and a CSS-custom-property-driven stack animation (`--toast-index`, `--toast-scale`, `--toast-swipe-movement-x/y`) for peek/expand/swipe-dismiss.
rankRationale: The richest Toast composition in the corpus: typed tones, a sonner-style promise() helper, and a full CSS-variable stack-animation grammar (--toast-index/--toast-scale/--toast-swipe-*). Feeds the Behavior (stacking/swipe) and API-composition sections; a story could recreate the peek-stack-and-expand interaction directly from its custom properties.

contextSummary: Network Canvas (a social-network research interview tool, academic OSS) is the only usage found that anchors each toast to a specific screen element with `Toast.Positioner` (`sideOffset`, `collisionPadding`) instead of a fixed corner stack, and focuses the toast itself on mount/blur to auto-dismiss — a rare 'contextual, per-toast-positioned' pattern rather than the common global corner Viewport.
rankRationale: Only usage found that anchors a toast to a specific on-screen control via Toast.Positioner (sideOffset/collisionPadding) instead of a global corner Viewport, and auto-dismisses on blur after auto-focus. A distinct 'contextual toast' archetype worth its own story and a citation in the Anatomy section for Positioner's optional role.

contextSummary: Bytebase's Toaster (a DB DevSecOps platform) portals its Viewport into a shared 'layer root' so toasts inherit the same aria-hidden/inert overlay stacking as the rest of the app — e.g. a 'session expired' critical-layer overlay correctly obscures toasts underneath it. Also i18n'd via react-i18next.
rankRationale: Demonstrates portaling the Viewport into an app's own layered z-index root so Toast correctly participates in existing aria-hidden/inert overlay stacking (e.g. hidden behind a session-expired critical layer) — first-party evidence for a 'Toast + other overlays' composition note, plus i18n.

contextSummary: Companion (Elgato Stream Deck / broadcast-switcher control software) wraps the toast manager behind an imperative `forwardRef` API (`show(title, message, duration, stickyId)` / `close(id)`) so the rest of a large Electron-style app can trigger notifications without touching React context — a common integration shape for retrofitting Toast into pre-existing imperative code.
rankRationale: Shows the common 'retrofit into imperative code' integration shape: a forwardRef wrapper (`show()`/`close()`) around createToastManager so a large pre-existing app can trigger toasts without React context plumbing. Useful for a 'framework-agnostic imperative API' composition note.

contextSummary: Cloudflare's official design system Toast. Notably has no user-facing variants but ships a documented `KUMO_TOAST_VARIANTS` style table specifically for a Figma-plugin generator to stay in visual sync with code — a design-tooling pattern worth citing.
rankRationale: Corporate design system with no visual variants by design, but ships a parallel style-constants table consumed by a Figma-plugin generator to keep design tool and code in sync — an unusual design-tooling pattern worth a callout.

contextSummary: The fullest Toast anatomy found in any registry: the only wrapper observed using both `Toast.Action` and `Toast.Arrow` together, i.e. an anchored/pointered toast with an actionable button — a distinct visual archetype from the more common corner-stack toast.
rankRationale: The only wrapper combining Toast.Action and Toast.Arrow — an anchored, pointered, actionable toast rather than the common corner stack. Feeds the Anatomy section's Arrow/Action documentation with a concrete visual archetype.

contextSummary: An unofficial Material Design 3 port that renames Base UI's Toast to 'Snackbar' (`useSnackbar` = `useToastManager`) and maps every part through a shared `createSlot` styling helper. Its own code comment flags a real Base UI typing gotcha: `Toast.Portal`'s inferred prop type references a non-portable internal module, so it hand-writes a portable `PortalProps` type instead — first-party evidence for a docs caveat on Portal's public type surface.
rankRationale: Renames the whole namespace to 'Snackbar' for a Material Design 3 port and documents, in its own code comments, a real Base UI typing rough edge (Toast.Portal's inferred prop type references a non-portable internal module) — first-party evidence for a typing/portability caveat in the props-guidance section.

contextSummary: Rosen Bridge (a cross-chain crypto bridge project) renders a full MUI-style `Alert` component (severity, dismiss, a copy-to-clipboard action button) *inside* `Toast.Content` instead of Base UI's own Title/Description/Action parts — a 'Toast as a bare positioning shell around our own alert design' composition idiom.
rankRationale: Renders a full pre-existing Alert component (severity, dismiss, copy action) inside Toast.Content rather than using Title/Description/Action — 'Toast as a bare positioning shell' composition idiom, useful for the composition-with-existing-design-system note.

contextSummary: NASA-AMMOS's Multi-Mission Geographic Information System (a real NASA/JPL mapping tool) uses Toast for map-status notifications, with `data-starting-style`/`data-ending-style` driving a translateY+opacity slide and per-type left-border colors. A credibility anchor: government/scientific software, not just startups.
rankRationale: A real NASA/JPL mapping tool (MMGIS) using Toast for map-status messages — a credibility anchor for the real-world section (government/scientific software, not just startups), with a clean data-starting/ending-style slide transition.

contextSummary: A card-game engine's Toaster: the cleanest, smallest possible faithful wrapper found (CSS Modules, one class per part, no extra abstraction) — good as a minimal 'first Toast' reference distinct from every other heavily-styled/Tailwind entry in this set.
rankRationale: The smallest, plainest faithful Toast wrapper in the set (CSS Modules, one class per part) — a good 'minimal reference' counterweight to the heavily-styled Tailwind entries, useful as a from-scratch starting point in the docs.

contextSummary: A standalone, sonner-compatible toast library ('the headless primitives era starts now') built entirely on top of Base UI's Toast — a downstream *library* rather than an app consumer. 7.4 KB gzipped, no !important. Description-based signal only; source file not inspected.
rankRationale: A dedicated downstream library (not an app) built entirely on Base UI's Toast with a sonner-compatible API — evidence that Toast is solid enough to re-platform an existing popular API on top of. Description-based only (source file not inspected in this pass); verify before citing API specifics.

contextSummary: Base UI components for React (shadcn-style) — fixes Drawer, Select, Toast and overlay/focus issues. Description-based signal only; no cached subpath search or file inspection has verified an actual Toast file in this repo.
rankRationale: Weakest-evidence entry: included for completeness from an earlier description-based pass, but no cached code search or file inspection has actually confirmed a Toast file in this repo. Keep at the bottom and re-verify before use in a doc citation.
